title: I Just Got a Micro-Internship With United Talent Agency. Here's How It Actually Went.
body:
I'm not going to pretend it was smooth.

When the kickoff call started and I saw the other people in the room, sharp, polished, the kind of people who introduce themselves with confidence and a rehearsed thirty second pitch, I almost talked myself out of being there. I work at Lowe's during the day. I run a small financial services LLC out of Cranford NJ at night. I study accounting. I don't have an entertainment industry background. I didn't go to an Ivy League school.

But I got the project. And here's why I think that happened.

## What Parker Dewey Is

Parker Dewey runs micro internships. Short, paid, project based engagements that connect companies with emerging professionals. No full time commitment. No months long application process. You apply, you get selected, you deliver work, you move on.

I was recognized as a top 1% applicant on the platform. I don't say that to brag. I say it because I was genuinely surprised. I'm competing against people with stronger resumes on paper. But Parker Dewey rewards execution, not credentials. That difference matters more than most people realize.

## The Project

United Talent Agency is one of the biggest talent agencies in the world alongside CAA and WME. They represent some of the biggest names in entertainment, sports, and media.

The project I'm building is a talent pipeline tool that pulls YouTube creator data to identify emerging talent before they blow up. The idea is simple in theory. By the time a creator hits 500k subscribers CAA has already called. UTA wants to find them at 50k, when the growth curve is steep, the engagement is real, and the relationship is still winnable.

So I built a working prototype before the kickoff call even happened. A tool that pulls channel data, tracks subscriber velocity, and surfaces creators based on growth trajectory rather than just raw size.

I didn't wait to be told what to build. I just built something and brought it.

## The Kickoff Call

I'm going to be honest. I was nervous.

Everyone else on the call seemed comfortable in a way I wasn't. They knew how to introduce themselves in rooms like that. I stumbled through mine. I didn't even mention where I went to school, partly because I forgot, partly because I wasn't sure it mattered.

What I kept coming back to was the tool sitting open on my laptop. I had already done something. Whether or not I sounded smooth on a video call the work was real.

The call ended. I stayed in it. That's the whole thing sometimes. Just not leaving.

## What I'm Taking From This

I'm an accounting and finance guy who taught himself enough to build a data tool for a major entertainment agency. That's not a resume line I expected to have at this point in my life.

But that's what happens when you stop waiting for the right moment or the right credential and just start building. The Lowe's job doesn't define the ceiling. The LLC I'm running, the licenses I'm earning, the projects I'm delivering, those are the story.

UTA is on my resume now. Not because I went to the right school. Because I showed up with something already built.

Matthew Coleman is the owner of MCC Solutions LLC, a licensed financial services firm in Cranford NJ providing bookkeeping, notary, and web services. He is currently a micro intern with United Talent Agency through Parker Dewey.
excerpt: I was nervous. Everyone else on the call seemed comfortable in a way I wasn't. But I had already built something before the kickoff started. Here's what actually happened when I landed a micro internship with United Talent Agency.
